refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly focus on the latency metric and its impact on safety-critical teleoperation, clarifying the performance requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link with minimal packet loss and jitter.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to operator within latency constraints.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use dedicated low-latency communication protocols optimized for teleoperation.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes near the worksite to reduce network hops.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation data traffic over other network traffic.
DP4: Employ real-time telemetry compression and efficient encoding.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Implement edge computing nodes near the worksite to reduce network hops.

potential_sources: Interaction with Safety Services for alerting on latency breaches
coupling_check.notes: DP2 logically isolates latency reduction by localizing processing, minimizing coupling with other DPs.

assumptions_risks: Assumes edge computing infrastructure can be deployed and maintained at the worksite.
Risk of increased system complexity and cost due to additional hardware.
Risk that network conditions may still degrade latency beyond control.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider fallback mechanisms if edge nodes fail or network conditions worsen.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Realization of teleoperation latency reduction via edge computing and communication management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles communication between edge nodes and vehicle/operator systems.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time telemetry data to operator interface.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as latency reduction logic and edge computing are core to Teleoperation module.
Vehicle module depends on receiving low-latency commands but does not implement latency reduction.
Safety Services monitors latency but does not implement latency reduction.
NGHE Solution is overarching but latency-specific logic is better localized.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency reduction DP is best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and maintain clear ownership.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Safety Services modules.
Risk of module overload if Teleoperation module scope grows too large.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and consider splitting Teleoperation module if complexity increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (edge computing) directly addresses the refined FR of latency reduction with clear performance impact.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if latency logic spreads into Vehicle or Safety Services modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as best IM boundary; interfaces are well-defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry and network link monitoring is feasible within Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency reduction responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Monitor interface complexity between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
Plan for fallback or redundancy in edge computing deployment.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy the requirement with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. Verification and ownership are well defined with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link with minimal packet loss and jitter.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to operator within latency constraints.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Edge computing infrastructure is deployable and maintainable at the worksite.
Network conditions conform to agreed parameters.
open_risks: Potential increased system complexity and cost.
Risk of network degradation beyond control.
evidence_needed: Validation of edge computing deployment feasibility.
